hyperedges.label: All provider guides follow the identical three-step setup template: 1) Create account and set up billing, 2) Create API key on provider platform, 3) Fill in ModelHarbor upstream key drawer with preset, key, and fetch models
Providers exposing both Anthropic-compatible and OpenAI-compatible endpoints for dual-protocol routing
Providers using OAuth-based authentication (browser flow or JWT signing) instead of static PAT keys
Provider guides in chunk 2 (8 presets with PAT auth and ModelHarbor Upstream Key flow)
Routing and resilience subsystem (circuit breaker + health probe + sticky + first token timeout + failure categories)
Five core concepts of ModelHarbor administration (Upstream Key, Public Model, Model Group, App, Consumer Key)

nodes.label: ModelHarbor — Lightweight dashboard-first LLM API router
nodes.norm_label: modelharbor — lightweight dashboard-first llm api router

nodes.label: Upstream Key Management — Add, rotate, monitor provider API keys
nodes.norm_label: upstream key management — add, rotate, monitor provider api keys

nodes.label: Public Model Management — Define public-facing model names and group into collections
nodes.norm_label: public model management — define public-facing model names and group into collections

nodes.label: Sticky Routing — Conversation-fingerprint binds to (upstream key, real model) pair for cache-hit improvement
nodes.rationale: Same conversation (system + first messages + optional user_id) prefers the previously used upstream to improve provider-side cache hit rates. Bindings expire after 1 hour.
nodes.norm_label: sticky routing — conversation-fingerprint binds to (upstream key, real model) pair for cache-hit improvement

nodes.label: Automatic Failover — Cool down failing upstreams and retry next candidate
nodes.rationale: Rate-limit, quota, overload, and timeout errors trigger brief cooldowns (15s-5min) before retrying the next candidate. Auth and permission errors stop immediately without retry.
nodes.norm_label: automatic failover — cool down failing upstreams and retry next candidate

nodes.label: Provider Adapter Pattern — Protocol differences isolated behind adapters; router core never sees wire-format payloads
nodes.rationale: Anthropic-compatible (POST /v1/messages, x-api-key) and OpenAI-compatible (POST /v1/chat/completions, Authorization: Bearer) adapters normalize errors into a shared taxonomy so the router can make consistent failover decisions.
nodes.norm_label: provider adapter pattern — protocol differences isolated behind adapters; router core never sees wire-format payloads

nodes.label: Shared Error Taxonomy — rate_limit, quota, auth, timeout classification
nodes.norm_label: shared error taxonomy — rate_limit, quota, auth, timeout classification

nodes.label: Quota and Cooldown System — Per-key counters by hour/day/week/month/total; frozen on quota_exceeded
nodes.rationale: Per-key counters track usage across time windows. When a limit is reached the key is frozen with reason quota_exceeded. Rate-limit and timeout errors trigger brief cooldowns.
nodes.norm_label: quota and cooldown system — per-key counters by hour/day/week/month/total; frozen on quota_exceeded

nodes.label: Security Defaults — Hashed keys, no prompt storage, HTTP-only signed cookies, login rate-limiting
nodes.rationale: Upstream and consumer keys stored as hashes (raw values shown only once at creation). Prompts and completions never stored by default. Admin sessions use HTTP-only signed cookies. Login attempts are rate-limited.
nodes.norm_label: security defaults — hashed keys, no prompt storage, http-only signed cookies, login rate-limiting

nodes.label: Consumer Key Authentication — Bearer token (preferred) or x-api-key header
nodes.norm_label: consumer key authentication — bearer token (preferred) or x-api-key header

nodes.label: Supported Client Protocols — Anthropic Messages, OpenAI Chat Completions, OpenAI Responses, OpenAI Models List
nodes.norm_label: supported client protocols — anthropic messages, openai chat completions, openai responses, openai models list

nodes.label: Tech Stack — Node.js, Fastify 5, TypeScript, Vue 3, Vite, Naive UI, Pinia, SQLite (libsql), Drizzle, pnpm workspaces, Vitest, Playwright
nodes.norm_label: tech stack — node.js, fastify 5, typescript, vue 3, vite, naive ui, pinia, sqlite (libsql), drizzle, pnpm workspaces, vitest, playwright

nodes.label: Design principle: Not a resale platform — intentionally avoids pricing, billing, payment, affiliate features
nodes.rationale: ModelHarbor intentionally avoids pricing, billing, payment, or affiliate features. Its sole purpose is to give a transparent control plane for LLM infrastructure.
nodes.norm_label: design principle: not a resale platform — intentionally avoids pricing, billing, payment, affiliate features

nodes.label: Project Structure — apps/api (Fastify gateway), apps/web (Vue 3 dashboard), packages/shared (protocol-neutral types), docs/, e2e/
nodes.norm_label: project structure — apps/api (fastify gateway), apps/web (vue 3 dashboard), packages/shared (protocol-neutral types), docs/, e2e/

nodes.label: Personal Access Token (PAT) Auth — Standard sk-... key-based authentication used by most providers
nodes.rationale: The dominant authentication pattern across provider presets: a static API key (typically sk-... prefix) is pasted into the upstream key drawer and sent as a Bearer token or equivalent header.
nodes.norm_label: personal access token (pat) auth — standard sk-... key-based authentication used by most providers

nodes.label: OAuth Authentication Pattern — Browser OAuth flow or refresh-token exchange (Codex and Coze)
nodes.rationale: Two providers use OAuth rather than static PAT: Codex uses browser OAuth with refresh token storage; Coze uses JWT signed with RSA private key exchanged for short-lived access tokens. This pattern handles token refresh automatically.
nodes.norm_label: oauth authentication pattern — browser oauth flow or refresh-token exchange (codex and coze)

nodes.label: Dual Protocol Endpoint Pattern — Providers exposing both Anthropic-compatible and OpenAI-compatible endpoints
nodes.rationale: Several providers (DeepSeek, MiniMax China, MiniMax International, Moonshot CN, Moonshot International) expose both Anthropic-compatible (/anthropic) and OpenAI-compatible endpoints, allowing clients using either protocol to reach the same models.
nodes.norm_label: dual protocol endpoint pattern — providers exposing both anthropic-compatible and openai-compatible endpoints

nodes.label: Three-Step Provider Setup Workflow — 1) Create account, 2) Create API key, 3) Fill in ModelHarbor
nodes.rationale: Every provider guide follows an identical three-step template: (1) create or sign in to the provider account with billing setup, (2) create an API key on the provider platform, (3) open ModelHarbor Upstream Keys, select the provider preset, paste the key, fetch models, and create. This consistent template ensures administrators can onboard any provider uniformly.
nodes.norm_label: three-step provider setup workflow — 1) create account, 2) create api key, 3) fill in modelharbor

nodes.label: Upstream Key Preset — Each provider has a named preset in ModelHarbor with pre-configured auth, endpoint, and model discovery
nodes.norm_label: upstream key preset — each provider has a named preset in modelharbor with pre-configured auth, endpoint, and model discovery

nodes.label: Fetch Models — Auto-discovery of available models from provider API for upstream key configuration
nodes.norm_label: fetch models — auto-discovery of available models from provider api for upstream key configuration

nodes.label: Key Shown Only Once — Universal security pattern: provider API keys are displayed only at creation time
nodes.rationale: Nearly every provider platform warns that the API key is shown only once at creation. This aligns with ModelHarbor's own security default of showing raw upstream/consumer keys only once and storing hashes thereafter.
nodes.norm_label: key shown only once — universal security pattern: provider api keys are displayed only at creation time

nodes.norm_label: consumer key hashing and prefix storage
